description: Add chaos/stress tests to permission-related side panel E2E tests. Each test file gets a new test.describe('stress') block with rapid-action, race-condition, and boundary-value tests. All chaos tests monitor for uncaught JS errors via page.on('pageerror').

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add a stress test.describe block with: (1) rapid permission cycling (Off→Ask→Auto 3x on plugin, tool, and browser selects interleaved, verify server state consistent), (2) override clearing (pre-seed per-tool override in config, change plugin permission, verify override wiped in UI and config.json), (3) rapid Select dropdown keyboard interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts
Rapid cycling test toggles permission 3 full Off→Ask→Auto cycles on plugin select, tool select, and browser select, then verifies mcpClient.listTools() shows correct enabled/disabled state
Override clearing test pre-seeds config with per-tool override, changes plugin permission, verifies echoSelect shows new value and config.json tools map is cleared
All stress tests use page.on('pageerror') to collect JS errors and assert zero errors at end
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
The prototype chaos tests (previously in sidepanel-chaos.e2e.ts, now deleted) proved these patterns work. The test file already imports selectPermission from helpers.js and uses createMcpClient.

Changes:
Add a test.describe('stress', () => { ... }) block at the end of the file. Define inline helpers:
```
const tick = (ms = 100) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));
const collectPageErrors = (page: Page): string[] => { const errors: string[] = []; page.on('pageerror', (err: Error) => errors.push(err.message)); return errors; };
```

Test 1 — rapid cycling: Use the same manual setup pattern as existing tests (mkdtempSync, writeTestConfig with reviewed plugin, startMcpServer with OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='', createMcpClient, launchExtensionContext, setupAdapterSymlink). Open side panel, expand plugin card, then loop 3x: selectPermission Off/Ask/Auto on plugin, tool, and browser selects with 50ms tick between each. After storm, verify pluginSelect shows 'Auto', echoSelect shows 'Auto', browserSelect shows 'Auto'. Poll mcpClient.listTools() to verify e2e-test_echo and browser_list_tabs don't have [Disabled] prefix.

Test 2 — override clearing: writeTestConfig with `permissions: { 'e2e-test': { permission: 'off', tools: { echo: 'ask' }, reviewedVersion } }`. Open side panel, expand card, verify echo shows 'Ask'. Change plugin to 'Auto', verify echo shows 'Auto' (override cleared). Verify readTestConfig shows tools undefined. Then set echo to Off (fresh override), change plugin back to Auto, verify echo is Auto again.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify existing tests — only add new describe block
- Use `import type { Page } from '@playwright/test'` for the collectPageErrors helper
- Follow exact same setup/teardown pattern as existing tests in the file

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid config changes in succession and permission flicker resistance.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts
Rapid config changes test performs 5 permission toggles in 2 seconds and verifies each propagates to side panel without UI lag
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file already tests real-time state propagation — permission changes reflecting without reload, auth toggle + tab state changes, browser disconnect/reconnect. But it has no rapid-action tests.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with inline tick/collectPageErrors helpers. Test: open side panel, change plugin permission 5x (Off→Auto→Off→Auto→Off) with only 100ms between each via selectPermission. After all changes, verify the final value ('Off') is shown in UI. Then change back to 'Auto' and poll mcpClient.listTools() to verify all tools are enabled.

Constraints:
- Follow existing manual setup pattern in the file
- Must use OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='' to make selects interactive
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for simultaneous tool calls and connection state thrashing.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts
Simultaneous tool calls test triggers two MCP tool calls in parallel and verifies activity border appears for both and clears after completion
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests connection status, tab state transitions, and tool invocation animation. It uses createMcpClient and startTestServer. The tool invocation test uses the slow_with_progress e2e-test tool.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with test for simultaneous tool calls. Setup with test server + matching tab so plugin is ready. Call two tools in parallel via Promise.all([mcpClient.callTool('e2e-test_echo', { message: 'a' }), mcpClient.callTool('e2e-test_greet', { name: 'b' })]). Before calling, verify the expanded plugin card shows activity border when tools run. After both complete, verify activity border is gone.

Constraints:
- The test server must be running and a matching tab open for tools to dispatch
- Follow existing pattern: openTestAppTab for the matching tab
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-error-states.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid permission changes during error state and HTML-safe error rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-error-states.e2e.ts
Rapid permission test changes permission 5x while error card is visible, verifies permission is persisted correctly
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-error-states.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file already tests broken plugin cards, error truncation, and permission changes alongside errors. It uses createMinimalPlugin to create test plugins and selectPermission helper.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with test that: creates a broken plugin (invalid specifier in config), starts server, opens side panel, verifies error card visible. Then rapidly toggles the e2e-test plugin permission 5x (Off→Auto→Off→Auto→Off) using selectPermission. After all toggles, verify final permission value in UI matches expected, and verify readTestConfig shows correct persisted value.

Constraints:
- Must create broken plugin alongside working e2e-test plugin
- The error card for the broken plugin should remain visible throughout permission changes on the working plugin
- Do NOT modify existing tests
